title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest signal is around pre-deployment evaluation and adversarial testing rather than new governance rules. The most relevant updates focus on simulating deployment behavior before release, probing jailbreak resilience, exposing multimodal agent attack surfaces, and improving evaluation and audit infrastructure for health agents, cybersecurity systems, and reproducibility.

items.title: OpenAI introduces deployment simulation for pre-release behavior prediction
items.why_it_matters: It could make safety evaluations more realistic by testing likely real-world interactions before launch.

items.title: Red-team study tests Anthropic Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 against automated jailbreaks
items.why_it_matters: It adds fresh evidence on frontier-model robustness against scalable adversarial attack methods.

items.title: Study finds multimodal hidden instruction attacks can evade agent skill scanners
items.why_it_matters: It highlights a concrete security gap in agent vetting pipelines that rely mainly on text and code.

items.title: RubricsTree proposes scalable open-ended evaluation for personal health agents
items.why_it_matters: Health-agent deployment needs stronger, scalable evaluation of memory and medical skills before clinical use.

items.title: Cybersecurity log dataset pairs ATT&CK labels with small language model evaluation
items.why_it_matters: Better labeled security data can improve benchmarking of AI systems used for attack detection.

items.title: ReproRepo aims to scale reproducibility audits using GitHub repository issues
items.why_it_matters: Reproducibility audits are a practical accountability mechanism for evaluating research and agent reliability.

items.title: Diagnostic software suite targets auditing of learned PDE simulators
items.why_it_matters: It advances auditing methods for learned scientific systems where standard accuracy metrics can miss failures.

items.title: LLM-driven extraction system targets illegal fishing, seafood fraud, and labor abuse tracking
items.why_it_matters: It shows a governance-relevant use of LLMs for monitoring compliance and abuse in complex supply chains.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
